qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via unauthenticated OAuth2 credential refresh endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns the OAuth2 credential refresh route using an attacker-controlled accessTokenUrl in axios.post, not the advisory's GraphCypherQAChain node forwarding user input into Neo4j Cypher execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: Flowise GraphCypherQAChain.run passing user input directly into the Neo4j Cypher execution pipeline without sanitization.
evidence: The advisory targets packages/components/nodes/chains/GraphCypherQAChain/GraphCypherQAChain.ts, lines 193-219, where GraphCypherQAChain.run builds { query: input } and invokes the chain against Neo4j.
The oauth2_ssrf finding reports an OAuth2 credential refresh endpoint SSRF through raw axios.post to an attacker-controlled accessTokenUrl.
A text search of the manifest qualifying finding folder found no references to GraphCypherQAChain, Cypher, Neo4j, Graph Cypher, CWE-943, or the prediction endpoint root cause.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so runtime tiering is not applicable.
